aronui.when_to_use: When you need to visualize the directory structure in an ASCII tree format for quick inspection of file hierarchy.
aronui.output_shape: A string starting with the directory name followed by a newline, then indented entries with tree connector characters (├──, └──) for files and subdirectories.

pitfalls.text: Path must point to a directory, not a file. Error when file given: 'Error: Path is not a directory.'
Path must exist. Error for nonexistent path: 'Error: Path not found.'
Access to certain directories may be restricted. On attempt with '.' in a protected context, error: 'Access denied: VAF program directory is protected'.
Depth parameter can be any integer (including negative or zero) – negative or zero depth shows only the root directory, which may be unexpected if a deeper view is intended.

procedure: Set the 'path' parameter to a valid directory path (string). If omitted, the current working directory is used.
Set the 'depth' parameter to the desired maximum depth (integer). If omitted, default is 2. Negative or zero values display only the root directory.
Ensure the path is accessible; avoid protected or system directories.
Call the tool to generate the ASCII tree output.
verification: Check that the output begins with the directory name followed by a newline.
Verify that the tree structure uses standard ASCII characters (├──, └──) for indentation.
Ensure all expected files and subdirectories within the specified depth are listed.
If depth is 0 or negative, confirm only the root directory name is shown.

predict_records.predicted: error: Tool will reject negative depth value, likely returning an error message.
error: The tool expects a directory path, but the provided path points to a file, so it will return an error.
predict_records.actual: error: Error: Path is not a directory.

predict_records.predicted: success: The tree will show only the root directory name with no contents, as depth=0 limits to the starting directory.
error: The tool will report an error because the specified path does not exist.
success: Will show ASCII tree of current working directory up to depth 3, starting with the directory name.

predict_records.judge_reason: Agent predicted success but tool returned an access denied error.
